title: Climate scientists aren't surprised by summer's record heat: 'We've entered a dangerous zone'
subhead: Record heat, historic drought and sweeping wildfires have marked France's 2026 summer. Yet climate scientists mostly see global warming's expected effects playing out. Only the rapid succession of heatwaves has caught them off guard.

title: Why 19 men have been charged in a French mass rapes case
Life in Kidal, Mali, amid pro-independence Azawad Liberation Front rebels
China's AI doctrine is a challenge for Europe
subhead: At the Shanghai World Conference on July 17, Xi Jinping unveiled a doctrine for organizing AI that could upend the global tech order and neutralize the EU's regulatory edge.

title: Iran bets on war of attrition against the US
subhead: As US airstrikes persist in Iran, Iranian authorities are, for now, avoiding a full-scale confrontation. Their strategy is to leverage the threat of global energy disruption to force Washington to reconsider its approach.

title: In Canada, great white sharks settle in for summer in the Gulf of Saint Lawrence
subhead: The species has become a regular and seasonal visitor to the waters of eastern Canada. Its growing presence is due in part to ocean warming, as well as to the abundance of gray seals, which are a key food source.

title: Bernard Arnault, a 'free-marketeer' who whispers in the ear of every president
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). LVMH's boss has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

Didier Deschamps bids farewell to French team after spectacular World Cup defeat
subhead: The coach stepped down after a dramatic 6-4 loss in the World Cup third-place match against England on Saturday. Over his 14 years in the role, he nonetheless managed to restore France to the pinnacle of world football.

title: In Paris café, terror attack survivors find community: 'People want to tell each other: I am alive'
subhead: After long days attending the 2015 Paris attacks trial, survivors and relatives of victims met in a bar to comfort one another. They have continued to gather every month, joined by victims of other attacks, to share experiences, advice and, above all, laughter and good company.

title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.
